---
title: "Souvik Das"
page-layout: full
toc: false
body-classes: home-page
---

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}
# Souvik Das {.home-name}

::: {.headshot-frame}
![](images/headshot.jpg){.headshot-img}
:::

::: {.home-links}
[CV](cv.qmd) ·
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=7MV40LIAAAAJ&hl=en) ·
[GitHub](https://github.com/piy4l) ·
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/souvik-das-048479153/)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="65%"}

## About

I am a 1st year PhD student in **Computer Science at Texas A&M University**. My work is broadly in **security, privacy-preserving machine learning, and high-performance computing**. I am affiliated with the [SPIES Lab](https://spies.engr.tamu.edu) and the [HipGraph Lab](#).


My current research focuses on **privacy-preserving Transformer inference (PPTI)**. I am interested in how secure inference workloads behave in practice, especially in terms of **latency, communication overhead, ciphertext-operation costs, and hardware efficiency**.

**Research Interests:** Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning, Cryptographic Inference Systems, Systems for Machine Learning, High-Performance Computing, Security and Privacy  

**Advisors:**  
[Dr. Nitesh Saxena](https://engineering.tamu.edu/cse/profiles/Saxena-Nitesh.html) · 
[Dr. Ariful Azad](https://engineering.tamu.edu/cse/profiles/azad-ariful.html)

**Email:** [souvik.das@tamu.edu](mailto:souvik.das@tamu.edu)  
**Office:** PETR 230 | PETR 246  

:::

:::

## Research Summary

My recent work explores secure and privacy-preserving inference for Transformer models. I am particularly interested in the systems aspects of encrypted and cryptographic inference, including:

- performance bottlenecks in privacy-preserving inference
- communication vs. computation tradeoffs
- hardware-aware evaluation of secure ML workloads
- practical deployment challenges in cryptographic machine learning systems

## News

- **Oct 2025:** Elected **Treasurer** of the **CSE Graduate Students' Association (CSEGSA)** at Texas A\&M University.
- **Aug 2025:** Started PhD in Computer Science at Texas A&M University.
- **2025:** Joined the SPIES × HipGraph research environment at Texas A&M.
- **May 2024:** Paper on container security accepted at **CLOSER 2024**.
